dialogueChunks.intro: The crisis broke overnight. {quest_stakes} is safe. The remedy worked.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The provider is still in custody, but {target_npc} organized a petition. Dozens of families signed it, including some who said privately that the ban was wrong. {quest_ally} is presenting the petition at the hearing. Nobody knows if it will work, but lives were saved and that's hard to argue against.
dialogueChunks.outro: They broke the law and saved lives, {player_name}. You carried the risk knowing what it meant. Some crimes are harder to condemn when you look at who they saved.

dialogueChunks.intro: The sick one is recovering. Thin and pale, but recovering. The deserter turned themselves in this morning, as promised.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They walked in with their head up and their family's hand in theirs. The tribunal is set for next week. {target_npc} is arranging the defense. {quest_ally} says several others have testified that leave requests were routinely denied.
dialogueChunks.outro: They committed a crime, {player_name}. They also saved their family's life. The tribunal will decide which matters more. Whatever happens, their loved one is alive. They told me: 'That's enough. That was always enough.'

dialogueChunks.intro: The food made it across. {quest_stakes} ate a real meal for the first time in weeks. The smuggler's friend sent a pressed flower back as thanks. That's all they could afford.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} says the embargo is losing support among the communities on both sides. {quest_ally} is connecting the smuggler with others who want to push for official relief channels. It's still illegal, but the tide is turning.
dialogueChunks.outro: Someone risked everything because people they loved were hungry, {player_name}. You carried the risk with them. Sometimes the law changes because enough people decide it's wrong.
